description: Ion MaybeFoldDiamondConditionBlock — Successor Count Check Admits Degenerate MTableSwitch, UpdateTestSuccessors toGoto() Cast Dereferences Switch Jump Table as Block Pointer. CVE-2024-3854 (sec-high). A type confusion and out-of-bounds read vulnerability exists in SpiderMonkey's Ion JIT compiler in the MaybeFoldDiamondConditionBlock optimization in js/src/jit/IonAnalysis.cpp. The optimization looks for a diamond pattern where an initialBlock branches into trueBranch and falseBranch that merge at a phiBlock, followed by a testBlock. When matching trueBranch and falseBranch, the code only verifies the number of incoming and outgoing edges, not the actual control instruction type. If an empty switch statement (MTableSwitch with a single successor) appears in one of the branches, it passes the check because it has one successor, same as MGoto. In UpdateTestSuccessors, the code assumes any block with a single successor ends in MGoto (asserts ins->isGoto()), but the degenerate switch violates this assumption. In non-debug builds, the MTableSwitch is incorrectly cast to MGoto, and the pointer to the switch's successors vector backing buffer is treated as the target block, leading to out-of-bounds reads when removePredecessor accesses fields from this garbage pointer. The fix (changeset cf42085d7c1d) refactors IsDiamondPattern to check that the last instruction is actually a Goto instead of just checking the successor count.
